description: Letter from Faraḥ b. Yosef, in Alexandria, to Yehuda b. Menashshe, in Fustat. Dating: May 12, 1069. Regarding shipments of gold and exchange of dinars for dirhams. Mentions the ships that sailed from Alexandria to the west, probably the first ones after the winter. The writer asks the addressee to drop a debt of 40 dinars that he borrowed from Abū Naṣr Thābit b. Saʿdān Baghdādī, a person who came to Alexandria in order to travel to the Maghreb, but missed the ship and returned to Fustat. Also Mentions details about the situation in Sicily and the battles there, especially the rise to power of the last Muslim ruler of Palermo, Ibn al-Baʿbāʿ. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 3, #520) VMR
description_he: מכתב מפרח בן יוסף, אלכסנדריה, ליהודה בן מנשה, פוסטאט, 12 במאי 1069, על משלוחי זהב, המרת מטבעות ועלייתו לשלטון של אבן אל-בעבאע
